title: EP0009 Daibutsu
fullTitle: Nara Daibutsu - The Great Buddha of Tōdai-ji, Nara

en: The Great Buddha of Nara—Rushanabutsu seated in the Great Buddha Hall of Tōdai-ji—is a colossal bronze statue that has watched over the city for more than 1,200 years. Even today, when you step into the vast wooden hall and look up at its gently lowered gaze, the scale and quiet presence are overwhelming.

Created as an embodiment of a prayer for peace in the realm during the Nara period, the statue carries within it countless wishes that have been offered over the centuries. Standing before its face, you sense not only the power of the statue itself, but also the breath of the people who have come and gone, hands joined in prayer.

On this visit, the hall was filled with soft winter light. As the rays streamed in from high windows and traced the contours of the Buddha's face and hands, the expression seemed to change moment by moment—solemn yet somehow intimate. Pressing the shutter in that stillness, it felt as though a single instant from a long, unbroken flow of time was being gently held in place.
ja: 奈良の大仏——東大寺大仏殿に坐す盧舎那仏は、千二百年を超えてこの地を見守り続けてきた巨大な鋳造仏である。広大な木造の空間に足を踏み入れ、そっと伏せられた眼差しを見上げると、その大きさと静かな存在感にただ圧倒される。
天平の世に「天下泰平」の祈りを具現化するものとして造立された大仏には、幾世代にもわたって捧げられてきた無数の願いが折り重なっている。その面前に立つと、像そのものの力だけでなく、手を合わせてきた人々の息づかいまでもが、かすかに伝わってくるようだ。
この日、大仏殿の内部には、冬の柔らかな光が満ちていた。高窓から差し込む光が大仏の顔や手の輪郭をなぞるたびに、その表情は荘厳でありながら、どこか親密なものへと刻一刻と変わっていく。その静けさの中でシャッターを切った瞬間、途切れることなく流れ続けてきた時間のうちの一瞬が、そっと留められたように感じられた。
